{{Quote|A thousand years ago, this world belonged to us. You are the intruders here.|Teneb Kel, to the Republic Governor of Begeren|<ref name="Blood" />}}
'''Begeren''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] region of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was originally part of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], which constructed enormous monuments dedicated to notable [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] on the planet's surface—a project that [[Death/Legends|cost]] the lives of an estimated hundred thousand [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]]. Following the Empire's defeat in the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], Begeren was claimed by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. The Republic allowed the construction of a gemstone quarry at the expense of the Sith statues, which fell into ruin. In [[3678 BBY]], the planet was [[Invasion of Begeren|seized]] by a [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|revived Sith Empire]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Begeren was the site of a [[Battle of Begeren|battle]] between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Look out from the balcony. Once, statues of great men stood before the cliffs. Those statues were monuments to warriors, alchemists… great philosophers who refined the sacred doctrines of the Sith.|Teneb Kel|<ref name="Blood" />}}
The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Begeren was located in the [[Begeren system/Legends|Begeren system]] of the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Worlds]] region—technically part of the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]]—of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Companion" /> It was one of the end points of the [[Kamat Aegit]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] that ran across the Sith Worlds region to connect with [[Khar Delba]].<ref name="Atlas" />

At least part of the planet was covered in rocky [[Desert/Legends|desert]], dotted with large, light blue constellations of gemstones. Tall cliffs loomed over the landscape, some of which had been sculpted into enormous statues of notable [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. Under the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], some of these monuments were destroyed to make room for a gemstone quarry. The Republic also operated at least one settlement on the planet.<ref name="Blood" />

[[Politics/Legends|Politically]], the planet was initially controlled by the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]],<ref name="Blood" /> based on the nearby planet [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]].<ref name="Atlas" /> Begeren was later claimed by the Galactic Republic, which installed a [[Governor/Legends|governor]] to oversee the planet. The world was seized by a resurgent [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]] during the [[Great Galactic War]].<ref name="Blood" /> Begeren would later fall under the hegemony of organizations such as the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]], [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]'s [[Zsinj's Empire|Empire]], and [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Atlas " />

==History==
===Sith world===
{{Quote|A hundred thousand slaves gave their lives to carve those statues.|Teneb Kel|<ref name="Blood" />}}
Begeren was seized by the Sith Empire [[Time/Legends|sometime]] prior to approximately [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]].<ref name="N1">Since the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] was defeated around this date per ''[[The Essential Atlas]]', they could not have colonized Begeren at a later point.</ref> The Empire set about constructing massive monuments of noteworthy [[Sith alchemist/Legends|Sith alchemists]], warriors, and [[Philosopher/Legends|philosophers]]. [[Slavery/Legends|Slave]] labor was used to build the statues, and an estimated hundred thousand slaves [[Death/Legends|died]] before the work was completed.<ref name="Blood" /> The [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]] of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Vacuus]] claimed the title of "conqueror of Begeren" when possessing a host [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] after his death.<ref name="Answer">{{TORcite|type=class|Sith Warrior|An Ancient Answer|Dromund Kaas}}</ref>

In the years following the Sith Empire's defeat in the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]],<ref name="Atlas" /> Begeren was claimed by the Galactic Republic. A governor was installed to oversee the world. At least one gemstone quarry was constructed to exploit Begeren's precious natural resources. This [[Mining/Legends|mining]] industry came at the expense of the Sith monuments, which fell into disrepair.<ref name="Blood" />

The world remained under Republic supervision<ref name="Blood" /> until the year [[3678 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The Old Republic, Blood of the Empire Act 1: Shades of the Sith]]'' places the [[invasion of Begeren]] 25 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Treaty of Coruscant]], which ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' puts in [[3653 BBY]]. Thus, simple math provides a date of [[3678 BBY]] for the battle on Begeren.</ref> when it was [[Invasion of Begeren|invaded]] by the resurgent Sith Empire as part of the Great Galactic War. Despite suffering losses, the [[Imperial Army (Sith Empire)|Imperial Army]]—aided by the Sith [[Darth Thanaton|Teneb Kel]]—defeated the Republic and killed its [[Unidentified Advozse Governor|governor]].<ref name="Blood" /> In the aftermath of the Imperial invasion, the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Lassicar]] was born to slave parents on Begeren, and when the youth discovered his parents were part of a [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] cell, he personally executed them and reported the cell to the Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Harvus]].<ref name="Lassicar">{{TORcite|type=codex|Lassicar}}</ref>

During the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] that followed the Great Galactic War, the Sith Lord [[Tytonus]] [[Tytonus's revolution|rebelled]] against the Empire and declared her own [[Tytonus's kingdom|kingdom]] on Begeren, earning her exile. Despite this, she hired [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] to supplant her own forces and landed troops on the Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]].<ref name="Friends">{{TORcite|type=faction|Imperial|Friends of Old|Dromund Kaas}}</ref> Also during the Cold War, the Sith Lord [[Darth Zhorrid]] maintained an [[Darth Zhorrid's Begeren estate|estate]] on Begeren, returning there at irregular intervals along with her estates on other worlds.<ref name="Zhorrid" />

While hunting for their missing ally, the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] known as the [[Hero of Tython]], three of the Hero's companions—the former Sith [[Scourge (Sith)|Scourge]], the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Kira Carsen]], and the [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] [[Archiban Frodrick Kimble]]—visited Begeren, though Kimble became separated from the other two while on the planet, and lost track of them as a result.<ref name="Jedi Under Siege">{{TORcite|exp=kotet|type=general|Jedi Under Siege|Ossus}}</ref>

===Galactic Civil War===
In the early stages of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Begeren was the site of a [[Battle of Begeren|battle]]<ref name="GG12" /> over the mines<ref name="FaD CR">[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> between the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. When the Alliance won, news of the victory spread as far as [[Klatooine/Legends|Klatooine]], giving hope to the native [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinians]] that they too might achieve freedom from their [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] oppressors.<ref name="GG12">''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]''</ref>

Following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], some [[Shadowfeed/Legends|shadowfeeds]] claimed that Begeren, alongside the planets [[Jaguada/Legends|Jaguada]], [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], and the moon [[Khar Shian]], were [[Scout/Legends|explored]] and [[Blockade/Legends|blockaded]] by the Empire, while some said these worlds were four ancient [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]] of the Sith.<ref name="FaD CR" />

==Inhabitants==
Under the Sith Empire, an estimated hundred thousand slaves died while working on Begeren's monuments. One of the Republic's governors was of the [[Advozse/Legends|Advozse]] [[Species/Legends|species]], while at least some of its soldiers were [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="Blood" />

==Behind the scenes==
Begeren was first introduced in a throwaway mention in ''[[Galaxy Guide 12: Aliens — Enemies and Allies]]'', a sourcebook for the [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]] published by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]].<ref name="GG12" /> It first appeared in the [[Star Wars comic books|webcomic]] ''[[The Old Republic, Blood of the Empire Act 1: Shades of the Sith]]'', published semi-weekly by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on the [https://www.swtor.com official website] of the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' throughout [[2010]].<ref name="Blood" /> ''The Old Republic'' included "Begeren Colony" as one of its server names at launch, though the server was eventually shut down during server consolidation.<ref name="TORst">{{TORweb|url=server-status|text=Server Status}}</ref>
